description: Nepali negation patterns for TTS text normalization and benchmark evaluation

notes.usage: Covers Nepali negation morphology systematically. Each entry provides positive and negative forms with natural sentences for TTS training and evaluation.
notes.coverage: Prefix negation (न-), negative particles (छैन, होइन, थिएन), polar verb pairs, compound/vector verb negation, and complex negation scope patterns
notes.importance_for_tts: Negation in Nepali interacts with verb morphology in complex ways. Prefix न- attaches directly to verb stems and can change prosodic structure. Distinguishing नचिप्लिँदोरैछ (negative reading) from चिप्लिँदो रैनछ (different negative scope) is a key TTS challenge.
notes.romanization: Romanization follows standard Nepali transliteration conventions used in linguistics

prefix_negation.description: Negation with prefix न- attached to verb stems. Covers infinitives (नजानु, नगर्नु), negative imperatives (नजाऊ, नगर), and participial forms.

entries.description: Negative infinitive: न- prefixed to जानु (to go)
Negative infinitive: न- prefixed to गर्नु (to do)
Negative infinitive: न- prefixed to खानु (to eat)
Negative imperative (timi-level): prohibitive command
Negative imperative: short form prohibition
Negative imperative (timi-level): prohibition on eating
Negative honorific imperative: polite prohibition
Negative conjunctive participle: without doing the action

entries.english: one who goes / one who doesn't go
entries.description: Negative habitual participle: characterizing someone who does not go

negative_particles.description: Standalone negative verb forms and particles. These are fully inflected negative forms of copulas and auxiliaries.

entries.description: Negative existential: the most common Nepali negation form
Negative existential honorific: used for respected persons or plurals
Negative equational copula: negates identity statements
Negative equational copula honorific: negates identity for respected persons
Negative past existential: was not present/existing
Negative past existential honorific: respected person was not present

entries.english: it happens, is okay / it doesn't happen, is not okay
entries.description: Negative habitual: something is not acceptable or does not happen
Negative definite future: asserts something will not happen

polar_verb_negation.description: Positive vs negative verb paradigm pairs showing systematic negation patterns across tenses and moods.

entries.description: Present habitual negation: stem + -दैन replaces stem + -छ
Present habitual negation with nasal vowel retention
Present habitual negation: note the chandrabindu in negative form
Simple past negation: -यो ending replaced by -एन
Simple past negation of irregular verb जानु
Simple past negation: vowel-final stem
First person present negation: distinct negative suffix -दिनँ
Second person (timi) present negation
Honorific present negation: used with tapāī̃-level address

entries.pattern: progressive: छ → छैन in periphrastic construction
entries.description: Progressive negation: negation falls on the auxiliary छ → छैन

compound_verb_negation.description: Negation patterns with compound verbs (vector/serial verbs) and light verb constructions. Negation typically attaches to the finite (light) verb.

entries.description: Ability negation: negation on the light verb सक्नु (to be able)

entries.english: is allowed to do / is not allowed to do
entries.description: Permission negation: negation on the light verb पाउनु (to get/be allowed)
Completive negation: negation on the completive auxiliary सक्नु

entries.english: started to do / didn't start to do
entries.description: Inceptive negation: negation on the inceptive auxiliary लाग्नु
Obligation negation: negation on पर्नु (to be necessary)

entries.english: has been completed / hasn't been completed
entries.description: Completive negation with intransitive auxiliary

entries.english: does (as a favor) / won't do (as a favor)
entries.description: Benefactive negation: negation on the benefactive auxiliary दिनु

complex_negation.description: Complex negation patterns where scope, morpheme boundaries, or prefix placement changes meaning. These are particularly challenging for TTS because orthographic differences are subtle but semantic impact is large.

entries.english: it turns out to be slippery / it turns out to not be slippery
entries.pattern: न- prefix on main verb with evidential रैछ
entries.description: Negation scopes over the main verb only; the evidential (surprise/discovery) reading of रैछ is preserved. The speaker discovers that the road is NOT slippery.

entries.english: it turns out to be slippery / (non-standard: negation on auxiliary)
entries.pattern: negation on evidential auxiliary rather than main verb
entries.example_sentence: चिप्लिँदो रहेनछ भन्ने कुरा गलत हो।
entries.description: Contrasts with नचिप्लिँदोरैछ. Here the negation would scope over the evidential itself, which is semantically odd. This contrast illustrates why negation placement matters for meaning.

entries.english: don't let (someone) go / let (someone) not-go (permit staying)
entries.pattern: negation scope: on light verb vs on main verb
entries.example_sentence: उसलाई जान नदिनु। (Don't allow him to go.)
entries.description: Negation on the permissive verb दिनु vs on the main verb जानु changes who controls the action. जान नदिनु = prohibition on permission. नजान दिनु = permission to not-go.

entries.english: without eating / impossible not to eat (double negation = compulsion)
entries.description: Double negation creating emphatic affirmative meaning: 'one cannot not eat this' = 'one must eat this'. Common Nepali rhetorical pattern.
Double negation expressing obligation: 'it has become such that one cannot not-go' = 'one must go to tomorrow's meeting'

entries.english: even if (one) does / even if (one) doesn't do
entries.pattern: concessive negation: न + past participle + पनि
entries.description: Concessive negation: 'even if you don't do it, it's fine'. The न- on the conditional participle creates a negative concessive.

entries.english: it is possible/appropriate to do / it is not possible/appropriate
entries.description: Impersonal possibility negation with मिल्नु. Differs from सक्नु (ability) in that it expresses appropriateness or external possibility.

entries.english: I hear what is said / I hear what is not said
entries.description: Negation inside a nominalized clause: the न- scopes only within the embedded clause. 'I also hear the things that are not said' (i.e., I read between the lines).
